meta.title: Two worked activation cases
meta.disclaimer: Public-source preparation for a Startup Partnerships work sample. Not a claim of existing Anthropic relationships or completed Motier/Station F programmes.

cases.oneLiner: Family-office VC with dense AI portfolio + La Maison founder venue — best fit for a repeatable evaluation cohort.
cases.publicThesis: Motier concentrates capital and community around AI founders (public: ~100 portfolio companies, nearly 40% AI; La Maison as venue for AI entrepreneurs).
cases.operatingModel: Portfolio access + founder community surface. Activation depends on a named programme owner who can recruit technical champions — not brand sponsorship alone.
cases.relevantSegment: AI-native or AI-enabled B2B product teams that need model/agent evaluation discipline before production spend.
cases.decisionMakerVsOperator: Decision-makers: Motier founding partners (e.g. Essayan / Houzé publicly associated). Programme operator: whoever owns La Maison / portfolio founder programmes — verify title before outreach. Do not conflate GP check-writing with mobilisation ownership.
cases.claudeSpecificNeed: Structured go/no-go on Claude for a real workflow (eval cases, baseline, cost/latency, security constraints) — not credits-only trials.
cases.proposedProgramme: Six-week Motier portfolio evaluation cohort using the Build with Claude kit: intake → representative cases → baseline → rubric → office hours → 30-day activation follow-up.
evidenceSupporting: Public Motier portfolio density and AI share (motier.fr participations).
La Maison positioned as AI entrepreneur community venue (TechCrunch / Motier communications).
Role mandate explicitly requires portfolio engagement, enablement, activation and ROI.
counterarguments: No accountable Motier programme owner → recruitment fragments.
Portfolio needs too heterogeneous for one cohort.
Credits create trials without evaluation discipline or sustained usage.
cases.successMetric: 10 qualified champions → 8 completed evaluations → 5 sustained activations (30-day qualified usage) by end-2026.
cases.killCriteria: No named programme owner after two warm attempts; or discovery shows no shared technical need across ≥50% of recruited teams; or fatal security/procurement mismatch for majority.
unknownsBeforeSpeaking: Who owns La Maison / portfolio founder programmes day-to-day?
Which 15 companies are technically relevant for Claude evaluation?
What shared blocker (eval, agents, RAG, cost) appears most often?

cases.oneLiner: Largest startup campus — programme owners matter more than the logo for technical founder mobilisation.
cases.publicThesis: Physical density of startups can be an ecosystem advantage; Station F hosts programmes and communities that can convene technical founders at scale.
cases.operatingModel: Campus + programme tracks + community operators. Access is through programme leads who already mobilise cohorts — not through a single campus tour.
cases.relevantSegment: Technical founders in AI / deep-tech programmes who need evaluation literacy before scaling model spend.
cases.decisionMakerVsOperator: Public face: Roxanne Varza (Director). Activation path: programme / incubator leads who own cohort schedules and founder trust. Treat director intros as paths to operators, not as the enablement owner.
cases.claudeSpecificNeed: A campus-ready evaluation workshop that programme leads can reuse: short briefing, case template, rubric, office hours — without requiring Anthropic brand as the invite.
cases.proposedProgramme: Station F programme-hosted Build with Claude micro-cohort (8–12 teams): one shared need cluster, two office-hours, documented go/no-go, product-feedback notes to Anthropic.
evidenceSupporting: Station F is a publicly documented high-density Paris startup campus.
Role mandate includes accelerator programmes and technical enablement.
Campus programmes already have recruitment mechanics; missing piece is Claude-specific evaluation discipline.
counterarguments: Logo-only conversation with no programme-owner hop.
Founders want credits/swag, not evaluation work.
Campus heterogeneity makes a single workshop useless without segmentation.
cases.successMetric: One programme owner commits; 8 teams complete intake; ≥5 complete an evaluation record; ≥2 remain active at 30 days; ≥4 structured product-feedback notes.
cases.killCriteria: Conversation stays campus-tour / brand-only after one redirect ask; or no programme lead will own scheduling within two weeks of first contact.
unknownsBeforeSpeaking: Which Station F programme has the densest AI/technical founders right now?
Who is the programme operator (not only the campus director)?
What prior AI vendor workshops worked or failed on campus?
